title: Cross-Fitted Circuit-Specific Pit-Window Priors
summary: F1 strategy guides repeatedly frame races in terms of circuit- and compound-specific pit windows.
rationale: F1 strategy guides repeatedly frame races in terms of circuit- and compound-specific pit windows. A shrinkage prior for expected stop timing by race/compound/stint/regime should give the model a stronger baseline than inferring every window from raw continuous features alone.
implementation_hint: Build out-of-fold empirical-Bayes encodings such as expected remaining stint length and `P(pit next lap | Race, Compound, Stint, RaceProgressBin, TyreLifeBin)`, with heavy smoothing and race-level group folds. Use deltas to the typical pit lap/window as new features rather than raw target encodings alone.
expected_effect: Adds interpretable strategy priors, especially for tracks where one-stop and two-stop windows are consistently narrow and compound-dependent.
risk: High-cardinality priors can leak badly if not fully cross-fitted; sparse race/compound combinations may collapse to noise without strong smoothing.
